name: Michael Jackson - Thriller
reason: The best-selling album of all time, revolutionizing pop music, music videos, and the music industry

name: The Beatles - Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
reason: A groundbreaking concept album that redefined what popular music could achieve artistically

name: Stevie Wonder - Songs in the Key of Life
reason: A landmark double album showcasing Wonder's genius across funk, soul, jazz, and pop

name: Adele - 21
reason: A soul-baring breakup album that became a global phenomenon with universal emotional resonance

name: Billie Eilish - When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?
reason: A genre-defying debut that captured the anxieties of a generation, winning all top four categories

name: Taylor Swift - Folklore
reason: An intimate indie-folk departure showcasing Swift's storytelling craft during the pandemic

name: Bruno Mars - 24K Magic
reason: A retro-funk tour de force that swept all six categories it was nominated in

name: Kendrick Lamar - Not Like Us
reason: A cultural-defining track that won Record and Song of the Year, cementing Lamar's legacy

name: Taylor Swift - Fearless
reason: Swift's breakthrough country-pop album that made her the youngest Album of the Year winner at age 20

name: Lauryn Hill - The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill
reason: A landmark fusion of hip-hop, soul, and reggae that became the first hip-hop album to win Album of the Year

name: Steely Dan - Two Against Nature
reason: A masterfully crafted jazz-rock album showcasing the duo's meticulous production and songwriting

name: Bad Bunny - DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS
reason: First primarily Spanish-language album to win Album of the Year, marking a historic milestone
